SheepClouds

A Chicago three-piece with two distinct songwriters, two lead vocalists, and the sound of a band built to be heard live. Driving guitars, heavy bass, steady drums, and melodies that stick. Debut EP "Places To Be" — coming mid-2026.

Book The Band

The Band

Three Pieces.

SheepClouds is built differently from most indie rock bands — they have two primary songwriters pulling in complementary directions. Daniel Ward's tracks are melodic, emotionally direct indie pop; Vicente Vargas's songs bring upbeat, guitar-forward rock energy. The result is a setlist that moves and breathes rather than sitting in one gear.

All three members play guitar, both Daniel and Vicente sing lead, and the rhythm section of Ward on drums and Cal Hensley on bass gives the band a locked, heavy-feeling bottom end. They're a three-piece that fills the room.

The band is currently refining four demos into a debut EP — "Places To Be" — slated for mid-2026. They're actively seeking live bookings, support slots, and festival placements across Chicago and the Midwest.

Vicente Vargas
Guitar · Vocals · Songwriter

Primary guitarist and co-lead vocalist. Writes the band's upbeat, rock-forward material — riff-driven songs with a classic rock sensibility filtered through a modern indie lens.

Daniel Ward
Drums · Vocals · Songwriter · Producer

Co-lead vocalist, drummer, and one of two primary songwriters. His tracks lead with melody and emotional directness — indie pop with hooks built to stay. Off stage, Daniel works as a music producer and professional audio engineer, bringing a producer's ear and a technical edge to SheepClouds' live sound and recordings. He handles live audio engineering for the band and has the chops to run sound for a full bill.

Cal Hensley
Bass · Guitar

The anchor of the rhythm section. Cal relocated to Chicago from Oklahoma and found SheepClouds almost immediately after arriving — jumping straight into rehearsals and locking in a low end that gives the band its weight. By day, Cal works as a nurse; by night, he's the reason the groove hits. A natural fit who made himself essential fast.

Music

Places To Be.

Places To Be EP · MID 2026

SheepClouds draws from two distinct creative voices. Daniel Ward writes the melodic, emotionally grounded indie pop tracks — "Care Enough" and "Better" — built around earworm melodies and introspective lyrics. Vicente Vargas brings the rock-forward energy with "Fast Lane Drive" and "Are We Still Friends?" — upbeat, riff-driven songs with the momentum of a band that wants to move a crowd.

Demo recordings available on request. Streaming release planned mid-2026.

Booking & Services

What We Bring.

SheepClouds is a low-friction, high-energy booking. The band arrives prepared and ready to play — and can make it work even when the venue can't provide everything. Daniel Ward brings professional live audio engineering experience, making SheepClouds one of the most self-sufficient three-pieces available in the Chicago area.

Live Indie / Rock Performance
Headline · Support · Showcase

Full three-piece live set drawing from a dual-songwriter catalog spanning indie pop and rock. Catchy, energetic, and stage-ready. Set length flexible: 25 to 50+ minutes. Comfortable in bar venues, small clubs, and festival-format stages.

Opening / Direct Support Slot
Tour · Local Dates · Regional Bills

Actively seeking to open for touring acts through Chicago and regional Midwest bills. Minimal changeover time, professional on and off stage, and a strong fit for indie rock, pop-rock, and alternative touring acts.

Own PA & Backline Available
Self-Sufficient · No Rider Required

The band can supply their own PA and full backline for smaller venues without in-house audio. Daniel Ward can run sound for the band and for other acts on the bill — making SheepClouds an unusually versatile and self-sufficient booking.

Private Events & Campus Shows
Corporate · Private · College

Available for private events, college showcases, and brand activations. Accessible and upbeat material with broad appeal. Professional conduct throughout, with experience navigating diverse event environments.

Stage requirements are minimal. Full input list, stage plot, and tech rider available on request.
